ground_truth: A clinical trial at Amsterdam UMC involving 2,847 patients found that AI-assisted pathology (PathAI Colorectal Classifier v3.1) improved early-stage colorectal cancer diagnostic accuracy from 91.7% to 96.3% and reduced reporting time by 38.7%. The trial, led by Dr. Jan de Vries and Prof. Maria Santos, was published in The Lancet Digital Health and received CE marking under EU MDR. Philips Healthcare and PathAI announced a commercial partnership to roll out the technology to 35 Dutch hospitals by Q1 2026.
summary: A two-year randomised controlled trial at Amsterdam UMC showed that AI-assisted pathology can significantly reduce diagnostic errors in early-stage colorectal cancer and cut reporting time by 38%. The findings represent one of the largest prospective studies of AI-assisted oncological pathology in Europe and have been described as a landmark in clinical AI validation. AI augmentation produced the largest accuracy gains among patients over the age of 70, with a clinically highly significant reduction in false negatives.
